profile.staging: GitHub Pages artifact plus exact same-origin hosted verification
profile.source: live code, current direct test attempt, game-loop review, application release gate, infrastructure debt sweep, and public-safe context

releaseGate.verdict: NO-GO / HOLD for launch; GO for local implementation
releaseGate.ci: Literal current last-five workflow evidence is 5/5 green; status and handoff still claim 3/5, proving a freshness defect rather than a CI blocker.
releaseGate.staging: Fresh same-origin staging receipt exits 2 at 3/10; health, manifest, revision, asset, repository, and launch truth are not joined.
releaseGate.cost: Cost-neutral field and content gates pass. Flat-rate plan cost remains notional and is not an alarm.
releaseGate.branding: Exact proprietary copyright passes 11/11 HTML files, but eight public routes display Studio text without the required VaultSpark link-back.
releaseGate.origin: Canonical health is 404; HSTS, frame protection, CSP, received-message evidence, and founder approval remain red external gates.
releaseGate.parity: Local responsive evidence is not bound to current HEAD or a working hosted staging origin.
releaseGate.approval: No launch or lifecycle-promotion approval is recorded.

gameLoopReview.evidence: Implementation review only: 31/31 targeted contracts pass. No substantive public SOUL, GAME_LOOP document, checked-in playtest corpus, or retention telemetry exists; no claim about fun, pace, churn, or sentiment is made.

recommendedMoves: Make session identity, PROJECT_STATUS mutations, and startup-brief freshness one self-validating observability authority.
Turn existing weekly command receipts into an Architect's Ledger that joins intent, execution, observed outcome, and the next adaptation without causal overclaiming.
Unify the next controlled action, season contract, and career legacy into one Three-Horizon Blueprint instead of adding another disconnected progression currency.

preverifiedSkips.reason: Deferred honestly: same-origin staging is 3/10, canonical health and edge headers fail, email delivery proof and founder approval are absent, responsive evidence is not same-revision, and registry SPARKED conflicts with local FORGE/S0 truth.

preverifiedSkips.candidate: Edit the authoritative registry, apex host, DNS, or sibling staging tree
preverifiedSkips.reason: Rejected as sibling-owned work. This repository may emit exact evidence and signed Ark cargo but may not edit another repository to make itself green.

preverifiedSkips.candidate: Consolidate all 243 server and local-runtime route branches in one pass
preverifiedSkips.reason: Premise is real, but a broad rewrite would exceed a safe parity surface. This session establishes a proven service-delegation seam first; future router extraction must move one command family behind representative state-transition tests.

preverifiedSkips.candidate: Add generic streaks, achievements, telemetry, or another retention widget
preverifiedSkips.reason: Rejected as speculative. The product already has return digest, legacy, tactics, inbox, challenges, rivalry, history, epilogues, and local feedback instruments; efficacy is unmeasured, so the audit joins existing authorities instead of inventing another reward layer.

preverifiedSkips.candidate: Claim soul fidelity or player delight
preverifiedSkips.reason: Rejected because the public SOUL is intentionally minimal and no real playtest receipt corpus exists. Executable implementation quality is not substituted for player evidence.
Rejected on evidence: package.json and lock metadata already resolve the declared Playwright version; only the local install is stale, and no dependency is needed for the ranked work.

secondOrderInnovations.premise: A persisted next-adaptation prompt has more loop value when it informs the next controlled horizon instead of remaining historical copy.
secondOrderInnovations.executionEvidence: Three-Horizon Blueprint now uses the latest committed ledger adaptation as the non-blocking Now milestone while preserving live command authority; focused derivation test passes.

secondOrderInnovations.premise: A bounded longitudinal summary can make franchise identity legible without fabricating telemetry or claiming tactics caused wins.
secondOrderInnovations.executionEvidence: Overview derives dominant declared tactic, explicit sample size, consistency, and observed film alignment from at most eight ledger receipts, with empty-state and non-causation language; focused tests pass.

items.title: Make session status and startup freshness self-validating
items.premise: The startup checker declared a Session 53 brief fresh solely because its date was current even though the authoritative handoff/status had already closed Session 53 and the brief still described the Session 52 closeout. Three scripts also bypass the invariant-enforcing PROJECT_STATUS write authority.
evidence: scripts/check-brief-staleness.mjs checks only generated-at age and returned FRESH for the stale session identity
docs/STARTUP_BRIEF.md says generated at Session 52 closeout while context/PROJECT_STATUS.json and LATEST_HANDOFF identify Session 53
scripts/detect-session-mode.mjs, scripts/verify-plan-mode.mjs, and scripts/render-startup-brief.mjs write PROJECT_STATUS.json directly
scripts/lib/write-project-status.mjs declares the single SIL-invariant authority

L1.recipe: Reject a brief whose rendered session does not equal PROJECT_STATUS.currentSession + 1.

L2.recipe: Export a pure startup-freshness evaluator that joins generated age, rendered session, status currentSession, and coherent marker; route every PROJECT_STATUS mutation through updateProjectStatus; add fixture and static-authority tests; rerender and validate a truthful Session 54 brief.

L3.recipe: Embed and verify a digest over every startup source so any truth-file mutation invalidates the brief without relying on timestamps.

verification: date-fresh but session-stale briefs fail closed
current session brief passes
all PROJECT_STATUS writers enforce SIL invariants
brief validator and staleness checker agree
items.executionEvidence: Pure freshness authority rejects session-stale/coherence-stale briefs; all three direct PROJECT_STATUS mutators use updateProjectStatus; Session 54 brief rerender, validator, and freshness checker pass.

items.title: Join weekly intent, execution, outcome, and adaptation
items.premise: The weekly command is transactional and tactical film is persisted, but command receipts are response-local and the product does not preserve one readable plan-to-result learning row per committed controlled week.
evidence: src/runtime/advanceWeekCommand.js returns commandReceipt and tacticalReceipt but persists only tactical film
public/lib/postCommitHydration.js preserves the response receipt only for the current refresh
franchiseCommandCenter ranks the next action but cannot explain how the prior command changed the next plan
game-loop review identifies the plan-act-learn join as the highest-value loop refinement

L1.recipe: Render the latest tactic and command receipt together on Overview.

L2.recipe: Persist a bounded deterministic Architect's Ledger for controlled weekly commits: record chosen General Manager decision and tactic as intent, exact started/completed authority, observed score/film alignment as outcome, and a non-causal next-adaptation prompt; expose it in both runtimes and render an accessible recent ledger on Overview with save/restore and transaction tests.

L3.recipe: Add season-level pattern summaries with explicit sample size and no causal claims, linked to film and decision receipts.

verification: only successful committed controlled weeks append
failed transactions append nothing
entries survive snapshot restore and are bounded
copy distinguishes observed alignment from causation
items.executionEvidence: Canonical footer contract now requires a valid HTTPS vaultsparkstudios.com anchor inside every footer; all eight missing routes repaired; Pages build and 11/11 public compliance pass.

items.title: Unify weekly, season, and career progression
items.premise: Tactical identity, the opening contract and owner expectation, season epilogue, and six-tier General Manager legacy all exist, but they render as separate panels with no single Now / Season / Legacy architecture or next milestone.
evidence: public/lib/franchiseCommandCenter.js owns the current controlled next action
GameSession dashboard exposes openingContractProgress and owner expectation
public/lib/tabOverview.js fetches and renders General Manager legacy separately
game-loop review scores progression 8.0 and finds fragmented presentation rather than missing mechanics

L1.recipe: Place links to existing weekly, season, and legacy panels in one Overview row.

L2.recipe: Create one source-derived Three-Horizon Blueprint with Now, Season, and Legacy lanes; each lane names its authority, current state, exact next milestone, and real destination action. Reuse existing command, opening-contract/owner, tactical-identity, and legacy sources; add no currency or predicted outcome; cover missing-state, browser, keyboard, and narrow-layout behavior.

L3.recipe: Add a season-close comparison that shows which declared blueprint milestones were observed, missed, or changed, without rewriting history.

verification: all three lanes derive from live authorities
missing sources degrade honestly
actions activate existing destinations
mobile and desktop present the same milestone truth
items.executionEvidence: Bounded transaction-only Architect's Ledger persists intent, command authority, observed result/alignment, and explicitly non-causal adaptation; rollback and snapshot tests pass; Overview renders accessible history.

items.title: Turn the dormant service bundle into one real delegation seam
items.premise: GameSession is 5,744 physical lines and constructs five services but calls none. ContractService's cap calculation also diverges from the production cap ledger, so the scaffold is dead architecture rather than a safe extraction path.
evidence: src/runtime/GameSession.js constructs this.services in constructor and fromSnapshot but has zero this.services.* calls
GameSession.getTeamCapSummary uses capLedger, team overrides, rollover, and deadCapCurrentYear
ContractService.getCapSummary uses league.settings.capHardLimit and team.deadCap and returns a different shape
existing tests assert service construction, not production delegation or parity

L1.recipe: Make ContractService cap summary exactly match GameSession and delegate getTeamCapSummary.

L2.recipe: Bind ContractService to the live session, move the exact cap-ledger computation behind it, delegate every production cap check through the service via GameSession.getTeamCapSummary, preserve the public response shape, and add override/rollover/dead-cap/snapshot parity plus a guard proving at least one real service delegation exists.

L3.recipe: Extract one complete contract mutation family with transaction, index-refresh, and adapter parity before deleting the in-class implementation.

verification: cap summary is byte-equivalent before and after delegation
team overrides, rollover, and both dead-cap years remain correct
snapshot restoration rebinds the service
all production cap checks use the delegated authority
items.executionEvidence: Now, Season, and Legacy lanes derive from existing command, opening-contract/owner, and General Manager legacy authorities with honest missing states, actionable destinations, responsive layout, and focused tests.

items.title: Enforce VaultSpark link-back on every public route
items.premise: The exact copyright contract passes, but eight of eleven public HTML pages render VaultSpark Studios as plain text without the CANON-006 link-back; the current helper and tests only enforce copyright/open-source language.
evidence: read-only release audit found links only in game.html, index.html, and landing.html
about, changelog, contact, ip, play, privacy, status, and terms lack a vaultsparkstudios.com href
scripts/lib/public-footer.mjs inspects only copyright and open-source claims
context/CANON_ADOPTION.md currently overclaims linked branding across all routes

L1.recipe: Wrap the Studio name in a link on each missing page.

L2.recipe: Extend the canonical public-footer contract to require a safe HTTPS VaultSpark Studios link-back inside every footer, repair all eight routes, and make Pages build plus public-compliance tests fail on missing, non-HTTPS, or off-domain branding links while preserving the proprietary line.

L3.recipe: Generate every footer from one manifest-backed source at build time and attest route coverage in deploy evidence.

verification: 11/11 public HTML files have the exact copyright
11/11 footers contain the approved HTTPS Studio link
footer navigation remains complete
no open-source claim or license file is introduced
items.executionEvidence: GameSession.getTeamCapSummary delegates to a live-session ContractService that preserves the exact production response shape and cap-ledger/override semantics; parity test passes.
